remaining_blockers: The DP still has to reproduce the full active objective, not only source-default classification.
Literal payload, item type, copy length, copy source, and copy length selection remain recipe/ledger dependencies.
Adaptive-count and tie-breaking behavior must be specified in any promoted active parser.
Row0/table origin remains entirely outside this source-state question.
summary.source_state_dimension_result: compressed previous-copy-end state makes source-state book-local prototypes tractable by proxy, but this gate does not implement or promote a complete active parser

summary.source_default_rule: previous copy source plus previous copy length if legal, else 0

scope.prototype_type: deterministic reparse recipes repriced with previous_copy_end source default ledger

remaining_blockers: Only cutoff 60 is repriced in this gate.

Non-source component counts and source counts are frozen from the train prefix.
It does not promote a complete active parser or a new compression bound.

decision.compression_bound_status: 8558.667 bits is the frozen validation scope, not final authorial method.

summary.interpretation: Item type is retained as a split-only forced-rule stream in the generation profile, but explicit operation type is not a compact recipe dependency once field shape distinguishes literal from copy.

predictive_validation.failure_rule: If prefix holdout gains vanish, classify as posthoc compressor. Family failures downgrade the result to partial predictive signal, not an authorial generation method.

counts_as_progress: A clearer ad-hoc dependency ledger for the fixed LZ recipe and row0.
Controlled rejection of row0-origin hypotheses with algorithm, cost, coverage, contradictions, and controls.
does_not_count_as_progress: Number of scripts or test rows.
Compression-only bit reductions without holdout or structural value.
Any semantic projection unsupported by CipSoft/in-game evidence.

hypotheses.contradictions: none inside inventory; explains no compact origin
hypotheses.negative_controls: not meaningful because this is the lookup baseline

hypotheses.algorithm: Finite-group / cyclic / hash-like formulas over pair indices and digit permutations.
hypotheses.contradictions: best exact row is classified lookup_disguise; hash rows are low-coverage and do not compress
hypotheses.coverage: 55/55 hits for the best finite-group row, but it uses one group per cell

hypotheses.algorithm: Local 2D propagation, quotient/orbit formulas, matrix seed overlays, and table-grid order rules.
hypotheses.contradictions: partial hits require many exceptions or posthoc overlays; no lossless compact grid algorithm
hypotheses.coverage: 21/55 best matrix hits; 15/55 best local-2D hits

hypotheses.algorithm: Derive pair labels/order from book usage, first-use order, symbol streams, or marginal signatures.
hypotheses.contradictions: train and stream signals do not survive as a controlled row0 generator
hypotheses.descriptive_cost_bits: not promoted; tested as accuracy/control signals

hypotheses.algorithm: Map pair labels to known lore/textual seeds or external phrase order.
hypotheses.contradictions: lore/textual seeds behave as searched symbol orders, not primary row0 evidence
hypotheses.coverage: 21/55 at best when lore-word symbol orders are allowed inside matrix search

hypotheses.algorithm: Treat row0 as an artifact of workbook export, SQLite ingestion, or local scripts.
hypotheses.contradictions: export scripts preserve source cells and do not synthesize a compact row0 formula
hypotheses.coverage: explains where the project reads row0-like tables from, not why CipSoft/game data has that table
hypotheses.negative_controls: manual code inspection of ingestion scripts; no generated table algorithm found

substrate_facts.note: These committed artifacts verify the code-class substrate used by the mechanical audits; they do not derive the 10x10 pair-cell labels.

what_remains_exogenous: Why those unordered pair labels occupy the 10x10 table cells.
A compact lossless algorithm for the pair labels after rule/search/exception costs.
A primary CipSoft/in-game plaintext, symbol table, or book->meaning crib.
A derivation of row0 from the active LZ book-generation formula.
what_row0_explains: Supplies the code->symbol substrate used to reconstruct the 70 book digit strings byte-exactly.
Supports unordered pair/mirror geometry and render-exception audits.
Allows the book-generation formula to operate on digit strings.

scope.fixed_recipe_limitation: All predictive tests keep the full-corpus LZ recipe fixed. They validate learned component scoring, not recipe discovery.
scope.reason_later_bound_not_used_here: The requested method change freezes the 8558.667-bit formula as the predictive-validation target and stops treating later compression-only micro-improvements as generation evidence.
